ontology.foodex2_description: Lemonade and similar lemon-flavoured soft drinks
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for fresh lemonade as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for beverages of this type is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of fresh lemonade (approximately 250 ml / 250 g) as typically served in a Singapore food court or café.

nutrient_source.title: Fresh Lemonade — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fresh Lemonade (cr_id G00010999). All per-100g values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Lemonade, commercially prepared — Glycemic Index entry; analogous estimate applied to fresh cane-sugar lemonade
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for freshly prepared house lemonade with cane sugar in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 67 is estimated from the closest analogous entry for commercially prepared lemonade / sugar-sweetened lemon beverage in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (67) × carbohydrate per serving (9.02 g/100g × 250 g serving = 22.55 g) / 100 = 15.1, rounded to 15.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar content (8.71 g/100g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage exceeds the 5 g/100g SSB threshold; insulin-mediated sodium retention elevates blood pressure risk despite low sodium (5 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid regular consumption of this sugar-sweetened beverage; request an unsweetened or stevia-sweetened version to eliminate the added-sugar-driven blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: 8.71 g free sugar per 100 g with zero dietary fibre produces rapid glucose absorption and postprandial blood sugar spikes; medium GI of 67 and glycemic load of 15.2 per 250 ml serving are clinically significant for glycaemic management
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage; if lemon flavour is desired, unsweetened lemon water or a stevia-sweetened alternative should be substituted to prevent postprandial hyperglycaemia.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral adiposity, and elevated cardiovascular risk; this beverage provides 93 kcal/100g almost entirely from free sugars with no protective nutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or eliminate sugar-sweetened beverages such as this lemonade, as habitual SSB intake is linked to dyslipidaemia and increased CVD event risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar with no fibre, no omega-3 fatty acids, and no meaningful anti-inflammatory polyphenols; fails the RA positive-criterion requirement for anti-inflammatory dietary support
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of this sugar-sweetened beverage; unsweetened lemon water provides vitamin C without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fresh Lemonade is a chilled beverage made in-house by combining freshly squeezed lemon juice, real cane sugar, and water. It is commonly served in Singapore food courts, cafés, and casual dining establishments. The drink has a bright, tart-sweet flavour profile with a refreshing citrus character. Nutritionally, it is a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) providing 93 kcal per 100 g, almost entirely from 9.02 g of carbohydrates of which 8.71 g are free sugars derived from added cane sugar. Protein and fat are naturally zero as this is a carbohydrate-based beverage with no protein or fat-contributing ingredients. Dietary fibre is zero. Sodium is low at 5 mg/100g. Despite its 'fresh' and 'house-made' framing, this lemonade behaves identically to a commercial SSB from a glycaemic and cardiovascular perspective. It carries a medium glycemic index of approximately 67 and a glycemic load of approximately 15.2 per 250 ml serving, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. It is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis without modification. A no-sugar or stevia-sweetened version would substantially reduce its clinical risk profile. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, halal, and kosher. Known locally in Singapore as 鲜柠檬水 (Chinese), Limau Segar (Malay), புதிய எலுமிச்சை பானம் (Tamil), and ताज़ा नींबू पानी (Hindi).
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in fresh lemonade?
Is lemonade good for high blood pressure?
Calories in a glass of lemonade
Is house lemonade a sugar-sweetened beverage?
Can I drink lemonade with rheumatoid arthritis?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Fresh Lemonade (cr_id G00010999) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Source match is exact. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which reduces GI confidence to medium but does not affect overall record confidence given the strong primary nutrient source. Local-language names are well-established translations for lemonade across Singapore's four official languages.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as this is a sugar-water-citrus beverage containing no protein-contributing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero as this beverage contains no fat-contributing ingredients; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source; lemon juice in diluted beverage form contributes negligible fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in HPB Foodo entry for this beverage. vitamin_c_mg set to -1: although lemon juice contains vitamin C, the HPB source does not report a specific vitamin C value for this prepared beverage entry; a future update should seek a vitamin C value from USDA FoodData Central for lemonade. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data not reported in HPB Foodo for this beverage entry. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for commercially prepared lemonade; no direct GI entry exists for freshly prepared cane-sugar lemonade. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = 67 × 22.55 / 100 = 15.1 (reported as 15.2 after rounding). This beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) for all clinical and regulatory purposes despite its 'fresh' and 'house-made' description. Multilingual local names added in iteration 3 to satisfy G6 and Q1/Q8: Chinese Simplified 鲜柠檬水, Chinese Traditional 鮮檸檬水, Malay Limau Segar, Tamil புதிய எலுமிச்சை பானம், Hindi ताज़ा नींबू पानी. Although Fresh Lemonade is a Western beverage, it is widely sold and consumed in Singapore and the validator requires Singaporean-context multilingual coverage for records tagged with region_tag SG.

q1_food_identity.notes: canonical English name present and multilingual identity satisfied

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: core macros and nutrient source integrity pass

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: alias coverage is adequate and multilingual alias consistency passes

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Added mandatory local-language names to local_names object: chinese_simplified (鲜柠檬水), chinese_traditional (鮮檸檬水), malay (Limau Segar), tamil (புதிய எலுமிச்சை பானம்), hindi (ताज़ा नींबू पானी). Added all local-language strings to flat aliases array and to aliases_by_language chinese, malay, tamil, hindi arrays. Added romanized_local (Xian Ning Meng Shui) to local_names and romanized array. Updated llm_training natural_language_description to reference local names. Updated notes to document multilingual additions and rationale. These changes resolve G6 failure, Q1 failure, and Q8 failure from iteration 2 validator feedback.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
